specializations: Fermentation-derived palm oil alternatives for cosmetics
Bio-based fatty acid production via yeast fermentation
Sustainable emollients replacing conventional palm derivatives
Biotechnology for deforestation-free cosmetic supply chains
product_highlights: Palmless™ — fermentation-derived palm oil alternative with identical fatty acid profile, produced without deforestation
Bio-based C16 fatty acids — precision fermentation producing palmitic acid for cosmetic emollient applications
Carbon-negative production pathway — net carbon sequestration achieved through yeast fermentation inputs
Transparent supply chain with full traceability from feedstock to cosmetic ingredient
notes: C16 Biosciences is a New York-based biotechnology company producing fermentation-derived alternatives to palm oil for the personal care and food industries. Their flagship Palmless™ product is produced by engineering yeast to ferment feedstocks into a lipid mixture with a fatty acid profile identical to conventional palm oil, eliminating the deforestation, peatland destruction, and biodiversity loss associated with palm cultivation. For cosmetic formulators, Palmless™ serves as a drop-in replacement for palm-derived emollients, fatty acid esters, and surfactant precursors, enabling deforestation-free formulation without compromising sensory performance or stability.
